title: Coelogyne datensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne datensis is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Coelogyne. This genus belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which comprises the orchid family. Coelogyne datensis is further classified as being in the subtribe Coelogyninae. Within the subtribe, the species is part of the tribe Epidendroideae. This classification reflects the species' evolutionary relationships and its position within the orchid family.
uses: Coelogyne datensis is traditionally used in various medicinal practices, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its roots and leaves are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating wounds and reducing swelling. Some studies suggest that extracts from this plant may possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which could be beneficial in preventing infections and promoting overall health. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and applications of Coelogyne datensis in medicine. Further studies on its safety and efficacy are also necessary to confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.
constituents: Coelogyne datensis contains flavonoids, which are a type of plant compound with potential health benefits. These flavonoids may include glycosides and other derivatives that contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. Research on Coelogyne datensis is ongoing, and its active constituents may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The specific composition of active constituents in Coelogyne datensis may vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growing conditions. Further studies are needed to fully understand the active constituents and potential uses of this plant.

intro: Coelogyne datensis, also known as the Coelogyne orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne datensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne datensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy in many cultures.
